channel.title: Vox English
channel.description:
I made Vox English to keep my English active. Each episode follows a simple format that helps me return to the language regularly: a short scene, a two-speed dialogue, an explanation, and the transcript. If this format speaks to you too, you can listen at your own pace.
To follow along while listening: https://www.skepvox.com/podcast/english/
channel.itunes:author: skepvox
channel.itunes:summary:
I made Vox English to keep my English active. Each episode follows a simple format that helps me return to the language regularly: a short scene, a two-speed dialogue, an explanation, and the transcript. If this format speaks to you too, you can listen at your own pace.
To follow along while listening: https://www.skepvox.com/podcast/english/

item.title: 001 - The Two-Minute Phone Call
item.description:
Complete guide and transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/001-the-two-minute-phone-call#complete-transcript
I use this episode to come back to a small but useful part of spoken English: how to keep a phone call moving without sounding abrupt.
The scene is a short neighbor call. A simple request becomes less simple because of timing, hesitation, and the pressure of someone waiting just outside the door.
On the episode page, I keep the complete transcript, vocabulary, and notes that help me return to the audio more slowly.
item.content:encoded:
Complete guide and transcript:
https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/001-the-two-minute-phone-call#complete-transcript

I use this episode to come back to a small but useful part of spoken English: how to keep a phone call moving without sounding abrupt.

The scene is a short neighbor call. A simple request becomes less simple because of timing, hesitation, and the pressure of someone waiting just outside the door.

On the episode page, I keep the complete transcript, vocabulary, and notes that help me return to the audio more slowly.

item.title: 002 - The Bowl of Something
item.description:
Complete guide and transcript: https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/002-the-bowl-of-something#complete-transcript
I use this episode to come back to deliberate imprecision in spoken English: approximate times, loose quantities, softened categories, placeholder words, and list extenders.
The scene takes place in a kitchen before a visit downstairs. A casual message becomes a neighborly misunderstanding about bowls, soup, timing, and how much detail a friendly invitation really needs.
On the episode page, I keep the complete transcript, vocabulary, and notes that help me return to the audio more slowly.
item.content:encoded:
Complete guide and transcript:
https://skepvox.com/podcast/english/002-the-bowl-of-something#complete-transcript

I use this episode to come back to deliberate imprecision in spoken English: approximate times, loose quantities, softened categories, placeholder words, and list extenders.

The scene takes place in a kitchen before a visit downstairs. A casual message becomes a neighborly misunderstanding about bowls, soup, timing, and how much detail a friendly invitation really needs.

On the episode page, I keep the complete transcript, vocabulary, and notes that help me return to the audio more slowly.
